preamble: The transformation of the voiceless glottal fricative [h] into the voiced labio-velar approximant [w]. This rare shift often involves labialization or is part of a complex reorganization of the approximant system.

examples.note: Historically seen in the development of certain clusters or in specific regional dialects (e.g. 'hw' to 'w').

sources: Wells, J. C. (1982). Accents of English.
